channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL — 53 replies — 212.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:30 UTC

OP

>AIIIIIEEEEEEE DON'T SELL

Early replies (first 3)

61924202
>>61924190
>DO NOT SELL
He literally said it kek

61924210
>>61924190
>Oil prices will be lower than ever
>do not sell
Wait what?

61924222
>>61924190
>Saaaaaaar DO NOT REDEEM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61924494
>>61924190
My son longed crypto.
Thank you for your attention to this matter!

61924513
>>61924270
>someone smother this fucking demented old retard
Rubio's too much of a cuck for that.

61924515
>>61924452
Orange Kramer

61924522
>>61924513
He's too short to reach over the top of the bed

61924558
Selling is now a federal offense according to some law Trump found from 1813.

61924600
Greatest president in history. Getting rid of evil Jihadists while keeping the stock market pumping.

Bless DJT

61924637
>day 3 of the 4 day special regime change operation

61924674
>>61924479
>The Ayatollah literally had 42 leaders prepared in case the Jews killed the first 41 successors at the same time as khamenei
I'm sure the 42nd guy down the line is going to be a great leader and will really stand up to a nation that spends double his country's GDP on their military alone.

61924691
>>61924190
DO NOT REDEEEEM

61924712
>>61924259
Reminder that dodging the draft is based and only niggers, faggots, and white trash join the (((military))).

61924715
>>61924637
Russia's only on day 1200 of its 3 day special Ukraine operation kek there's so much more to go

61924755
>>61924674
price != value
i thought /biz/ would understand basic economic concepts

61924758
>>61924190
we are so fucked it is unreal

61924762
>>61924190
DONT SELL KEK

61924893
so sell

61924904
>>61924210
i too am confused, i think he means everything with oil as an input
but then he is legitimately a senile moron who never read a book soooo... idk.

61926608
>>61924190
DO NOT SELL
STOP SELLING
YOU WILL FUCK THE ECONOMY REEEEE

61926626
>>61924270
Cope, seethe, dial8, YWNBAW et cetera. Oh, and 3 more years. Oh oh, and I voted for this.

61927505
>>61924479
>>61924674
Iran has spent the past 30 years preparing for this day, for them it's existential, it's the reason they wanted nukes. They may ultimately be destroyed but they are not going to surrender or show restraint.

61928137
>>61924190
One month special military operation

61928146
>>61924237
>if the oil industry collapses
Why do zoomers say the most retarded shit, ah hell we were all young and dumb once I guess.

61928151
>>61924190
LOL
https://sonar21.com/is-donald-trump-looking-for-an-exit-ramp/

61928154
>>61928151
EXIT STRATEGY.
USA LOSS THE WAR

61928163
>>61924350
>>61924412
If you're talking about anything except the Epstein files, child rape, blood rituals and mossad then this exercise has been a success

61931180
>>61924190
How is cult spinning this?

61931265
>>61926626
YWNBAJ

61931328
>>61924674
They didn't even get the 2nd guy. They got none of the most key people in Iran other than Khamenei - Arafi, Ejei, Larijani, Pezeshkian, Ghalibaf.

61931914
>>61924412
And you would be right, kek.
>When pressed on his plans for a transition of power, Mr. Trump said he hoped Iran’s elite military forces — including hardened officers of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps who have held substantial influence and profited from the existing regime — would simply turn over their weapons to the Iranian populace.
>“They would really surrender to the people, if you think about it,” he said.

61931961
>>61924190

item.title: Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock — 43 replies — 149.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Passive Money Farming / Side Hustles While on the Clock

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:25 UTC

OP

>Have an easy office job
>Mostly just scroll on my phone
>Literally just do barely anything and make salary
>Realize I could make money in my phone during this time
>Start making AI art, get up to $100 a month from subs
Anyone aware of something that could make more than this and is primarily done on your phone. I do not mind doing it on my PC at home too, as I already do this with AI stuff For now, AI is easy enough and extra money is extra money but more cash flows will always be better
Note, I want actual income, not “Just sell options on your phone while at your job bro”

Early replies (first 3)

61924922
So basically asian tits make me straight haha

61924932
I'm considering getting a 3d printer farm plus I know how to work with the files and make custom things but idk seems effort

61924936
>>61924922
I’m happy for you anon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61925623
>>61925611
Oops meant high six figure.

61925624
>>61925611
Well the numbers are based on how much you can extract monthly while still living like a human and not like a nigger so yeah

61925650
I was considering candle making but it's saturated by women

61925663
Hey man, I know it's difficult, but listen, the honest truth is that most phone based side hustles that feel easy are capped low and crowded, so your hundred a month from AI art is actually normal, not underperformance, and the uncomfortable reality is that meaningful extra income usually comes from either building an audience, selling something people repeatedly buy, or doing work others are too lazy or too skilled to do, which means if you want to beat that number from your phone you should focus on things like running niche subscription pages with consistent content drops, flipping underpriced items locally through marketplace apps, managing short form content pages that you later monetize with affiliates, or doing simple but in demand freelance tasks through mobile friendly platforms, because the people who quietly make real side money are not chasing magic passive tricks, they are stacking small repeatable systems and letting them compound while everyone else keeps searching for effortless cash.

61925701
>>61925560
I’m a doctor and found a woman similiar to that but white. I already left her tho because i want to fuck differebt kinds of hotties. So whatevet doctor makes + social status.

61925705
>>61924882
i fucking NEED a gf this hot
how do i do it ? money? looksmaxing?
please someone tell me how

61925774
>>61925705
All you’re going to notice is that boobs are just blobs of fat that smell like fat and look way better than they feel. Also the woman will naturally be rather unimpressed by anything because of how hot she sees herself.

61925778
>>61925705
She's not hot, she just has big tits

61925779
>>61925564
Don't ever say dumb shit like that ever again. Lack of money destroys even the strongest love.

61925806
>>61924882
Making money off of a phone alone just means you are a content creator. Which is a viable side hustle if you have charisma and/or skills and knowledge. Everyone wants a job where they make money with their phone so try something else.

And it's nice having a big titty wife.

61925811
>>61924882
I must rub my cock on a traditionally conservative nubile large breasted Nippon whore

61925831
>>61925774
i dont care all women are whores anyway so i might as well have a hot big titted gf
>>61925778
you are either a homosexual or a jealous roastie

61925844
>>61925705
>>61925774
You don't NEED one anon. Remember you will find no peace with a "hot" woman. Every guy even your friends will be constantly trying to fuck her or messaging her. This will keep you in a state of perpetual paranoia and misery. Hot women are mostly unhinged and mentally unstable. They expect you to do everything in the relationship while offering very little in return. Probably boring unenthusiastic sex too. She will most likely abuse you in the relationship because she sees you as wasting her time when she could be out fucking other chads and gaining maximum attention for her herself. Hot women are out for themselves they know that they have millions of thirsty guys who will fuck them, and they will make you aware of that every day.

61925851
>>61925663
great post

61925903
>>61925806
I'm thinking of side hustle streaming and photography while travelling amerilard on trailer but doing so would require some investment

61925909
>>61925844
nigger i did the average girl route for 10+ years already both roads lead to the same destination at least with the hot girl you get to enjoy it while it lasts

61925926
>>61924922
Asians don't count as straight.

61925995
>>61925844
>>61925909
hot women are also extremely boring and shallow since they don't need to develop social skills

61925999
>>61925995
Wow they're just like me then.

61926357
>>61925508
patreon? how do people pay for ai anime girls

61926427
>>61925508
Based. I've been making some good money with 3D Asians. My IG has been blowing up recently with followers.

61926854
>>61925663
Thanks for the rundown
I’m fairly content and have a friend who is making more so if I can scale to 150 or 200 monthly that’s really not bad all things considered

61926904
>>61926427
Brother what the holy hell is this

61926915
I didn't read shit bitch. I just saw tits.

61926931
>>61926904
Slop that's been going viral and blowing up my social media.

61927004
>>61926931
seems a bit sus but go off

61928037
>>61926931
you're not making real money posting this shit on Instagram. Fuck off thirdie

61929784
>>61924932
You can make a shitload of money printing Glock switches for ghetto niggers in Chicongo.

61931922
>>61925650
There's a lot of youtube videos teaching women how to make their own candles and soap
>>61926427
what do you sell the followers after giving them big titties?

61931935
>>61924922
that’s gay

item.title: Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:41 UTC

OP

Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Early replies (first 3)

61931291
This is just a coon who doesn't understand investments and compounding returns.

61931302
>>61931288
Probably bought a bunch of houses and cars that he couldn't afford the maintenance and taxes on

61931311
>>61931302
>>61931291
>>61931288
He's right though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61931312
>>61931288
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

61931322
>>61931311
>Invest
That means buying jewelry to football americans

61931324
>>61931288
>nigger whose unique talent is to throw and catch pigs bladder manages money

sage

61931356
>>61931288
Nigga, you gotta have the discipline to do more than a single generation of wealth. Study the legendary Andrew Carnegie to learn how. That dude was legit one of the wealthiest men of all time and he was frugal in almost everything he ever did.

61931362
>>61931302
What really kills these guys is they feel obligated to support their “entourage” from the hood they grew up in before they made it. Not only support them, but also “invest” in their harebrained business ideas because they knew them from back in the day. Many such cases.

61931374
>>61931311
he’s right. it sounds like a lot until you factor in the expenses like DJs and strippers for your weekly parties, dom perignon for all the guests at said parties, of course a mansion for those parties to take place at, bling to show off at those parties, and a rolls royce to show up in (even though it’s at your house), etc.

61931384
>the kickers and punters end up being the richest NFL players after all is said and done

61931400
>>61931288
A billion isn't enough if you're going to live like a degenerate kingpin/try to keep up with the people who have literal infinitymoney (they're in charge of printing it)

61931445
nigga thought he could go full charlie sheen without charlie sheen money...even charlie sheen went broke

61931448
>>61931288
>100 million NW is not generational wealth
If you're a nigger even billions wont make a difference.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberia

61931452
>>61931311
>flaunt
There it is. I don’t even know how you spend that much money outside of real estate.

61931673
>>61931452
>flaunters
Add it to the list

61931780
If a league pays a salary only because they have to, and is designed that once you’re no longer employed to “go broke” what is the real issue.

Look at the statistics unemployed professional athletes bankruptcy rate says one thing. Bankruptcy is part of the design.

61931805
he chose to get paid in bitcoin and it looks like that was a bad idea.

61931885
>>61931780
>Bankruptcy is part of the design.
>giving people millions of dollars is designed to make them bankrupt
do they point a gun at the nigs' heads and force them to buy mansions, bentleys, cookers, and rent out half of da club every weekend?

61931892
>>61931780
>pays a salary because they have to
As opposed to what, slavery?

61931960
>>61931384
Discarding the fact that they're 95% white and many are non americans they also typically have the longest careers. A problem most athletes have with money is that they have no long term vision because theyre still retarded kids and dont understand they wont make any more money after they get cut which is typically under 5 years so they live a millionaire lifestyle without a way to maintain it long term. For the special team players their paychecks will continue beyond their retarded 20s so theyll be able to be fine financially even if they waste most of their first contract. The long career also gives them them longer nfl healthcare. The healthcare extends 7 years beyond the end of your career so rivers and his 12 kids got to be be covered until 2032 by playing a few extra games this year

61931967
>>61931311
i dont watch niggerball. how does 5 year $100M contract turn into a 5 year $60M contract? the rest of his explanation makes sense: taxes, acting like a coon, etc.

61931992
>>61931967
It turns to $60m because of taxes and agents

61932006
>>61931288
>naaah nigguh I want that Lambo fuck your index fund

item.title: What's the best high yield savings account right now? — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's the best high yield savings account right now?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:36 UTC

OP

What's the best high yield savings account right now? Mine just went from 4% to 3.3% APY, so looking to change

Early replies (first 3)

61930464
>>61930455
idk

61930660
>>61930455
marcus has some cds that are at or near 4, some are no penalty

61930673
>>61930455
It doesn't matter, savings accounts are a meme. You can make 4% in a day with one good trade.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930724
>>61930673
>just roll the dice my guy, it'll pay off.
what a stupid fucking nigger

61930844
>>61930673
you can also make -20% with one bad trade, which isn’t possible with a hysa

61931040
>>61930455
swvvx is a money market mutual fund that was paying 4.12% last week, but it fuctuates.

I keep my "free cash" in that just so it will be available within 24 hours if I need it.

For high yield, I have a basket of MREITS that pay crazy monthly divvies but I rotate through them, buying them when they are low and holding.

My favorite is DX - pays like 15% but I only buy when it is under $12.50 and it is around 14 now.

Also: GLAD - pays 9% and is at 52 week lows so just sold some DUKE (3.5% dividend) because it is up 30% from where I bought it and am rolling that into GLAD

Gladstone has a suite of financing companies and a REIT so you can check them out. They are fairly stable over time and pay around 7-10% each.

I think these financing companies are a good buy right now because the lend on long duration (often with pre-payment penalties) and borrow at short duration. This means that if rates drop, they will still be getting 6-9% on their loans, but paying 1-3% on their funds Since their capital is leveraged like 90%, that means they are actually making like 25-40% RoC.

61931043
The best high yield savings account to use is a brokerage and sgov or some other money market fund.

61931066
>>61931040
>>61931043
Does it matter that there's no FDIC insurance on these compared to a HYSA?

61931085
>>61930455
A CD

61931106
>>61931066
yes it matters.

that's why you are getting a high yield.
>risk = >interest/divs/etc

61931133
>>61930455
https://www.pibank.com/pibank-savings/
4.6%, doubt it’ll last long though

61931195
>>61930455
Blackrock ishare etfs, you can choose the term length of what bonds are in the etf. Schwab swvvx sucks because it pays so erratically.

61931216
>>61931066
SGOV is literally backed by the US military

61931263
>>61931106
>>61931216
Hmm looks like sgov is exempt from state income tax? That's even more money saved.

>risk
is it that risky if it's backed by short term US T-Bills? I would think it's crazy safe, especially for just keeping some money around for dips, maybe like $50k tops.

>>61931040
hmm looks like the dividends are fully taxable at the state level.

>>61931085
but then you're locking your money up. You give up liquidity.

>>61931133
found picrel for 4.75% FDIC insured. Is there any real catch with these banks? I have Sofi and never had an issue while getting 4-4.5% APY the last year.

>>61931195
yeah, these look good for a long term hold, but how do the returns compare to sgov? Looks like sgov is more flexible, but might do a mix of both

61931476
>>61931066
No it doesn't matter because a broker isn't going to invest your assets like a bank does. If the assets are in a brokerage, you own them. In a bank they are a liability on the bank's balance sheet. This is the ultimate reason not to hold anything in a bank account. It only takes 2 days to get money out of my brokerage into my checking.
>>61931106 is wrong.

61931483
>>61931066
Also, if the brokerage fails, the insurance is more than what you would get from the FDIC

61931574
>>61931476
So, you hold everything in a brokerage like Fidelity? I'm guessing a small amount to pay the bills in a checking, that's it? Because that's how I'm thinking to do things from now on.

61931837
>>61931263
That's true. I didn't know you needed liquidity.

61931879
>>61931837
Yeah, I'd want to buy something if there were a market crash outside of my usual dca, but in general I'd also want an emergency fund of 20k min.

61931954
>>61931879
https://fundresearch.fidelity.com/mutual-funds/summary/316175108

Even the MMFs are down to 3.55.

item.title: "Networking" is a waste of time — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
"Networking" is a waste of time

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:38 UTC

OP

> If you try to network with the rich, they'll laugh at you and go back to their ballgame "DON'T TALK ABOUT WORK PLEASE!"
> Try to network within your friends or family, "Yeah sure I know such and such and they'll let you know!" and you never hear anything back again
> Go to job fairs; "THANK YOU FOR YOUR RESUME! *shakes your hand* YOU CAN APPLY ONLINE AT WWW....NEXT!!!"

Where's the REAL advice on how to make money for once, outside of the memes "get a job" (which btw there are no real entry level jobs anymore) or "just gamble bro" (and end up in the 90%+ lost money statistic).

Sigh.

Early replies (first 3)

61930524
>>61930491
You join forces with other 4channers at a meetup in Miami.

61930527
>>61930491
true

61930539
>>61930491
anon, networking is a euphemism for "sucking cock". You want a job but have little to no experience? go to a bingo night for the elderly. Find the most homosexual old boomer faggot you can find and flirt with him. Be clear adn direct: I'll suck your cock for a job or penetrate you at your whim. Please. Then you land the job. You never noticed Bingo nights are full of 20 year old femboys flirting with old men. Of coarse not, you won't be asking retarded questions about networking here otterwise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61930630
>>61930491
As head of recruitment, I don't bother with interviewing potential employees. I throw them into a trial networking event and observe how they react. Stiff, stuffy, scared? No thanks. If you don't finish the event energised, at least a dozen business cards in your hand, you're not the candidate for us.

61930680
>>61930491
Networking is for students and extroverts. Go to higher education and go to all the parties. The old boy network is real.

61930745
>>61930630
Why even LARP about this like what’s your reasoning?

61930769
>>61930524
tell me more

61930781
>>61930630
stfu u fake the way to win a networking event is fine one company there that has one or two people who are interested in you. but networking job fairs etc are all scams. the only way in is to lie on your resume like everyone else.

61930871
>>61930491
Networking is great if you're hot. I'm very attractive, athletic and can phone in charisma if I need to (Im socially awkward otherwise).

the rub is you need to be fit, dress well and have an aura to yourself. Want women? Be hot. Want money? Be hot. It's that simple, just be attractive. I think everyone has some control over this in some sense. You need charisma, but you can get away with having less of it if you're hotter but if you have both you will see lots of benefits. That has been my experience. I hate networking but I want to be rich so I need to get back on the horse and get out there again...

61930897
>>61930524
Im in Miami there's no one there who knows anything about this place

61930957
>>61930769
>>61930897
I just think there should be a secret meetup of 4channers at a Hilton in Miami. Pick the date, but don't give a time or exact meetup point. Then have a special color or code to signal you are a fellow 4channer.

Then we can truly discuss business and start building an empire.

61931017
>>61930957
I've done this, you don't have to be secretive about it. Feds will find it anyway, just don't make the point of the group political action and you'll be fine. With enough anons you can do anything.

61931411
Networking is an euphemism for sex work. It really means free blow jobs or handjobs.

61931421
>>61930491
Did you fall for the not going to college and joining a frat meme?

61931459
>>61930491
it's overrated. if you're just quiet and keep your head down, you'll be able to blow the whistle on all the "networking" going on when the time is right.

61931535
>>61931017
I mean literally for business. 4chan bans actually business collaboration on the board for some reason.

61931936
>>61931535
4chan is the place to signal meets either offline (preferred) or in a more private location (chat). It's not a place for organizing anything serious.

61931991
I've spent so much time building infrastructure in 100% independence to where my independent lifestyle compounds on itself. I'm too powerful and scare the Network Gen Xer with the audacity of my existence being a thing.

Blood magic

item.title: Iran mines Bitcoin at $1,320 per coin. Sells it at $68,000. A 50x profit margin using state subsidized electricity. 700,… — 16 replies — 47.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Iran mines Bitcoin at $1,320 per coin. Sells it at $68,000. A 50x profit margin using state subsidized electricity. 700,…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:22 UTC

OP

Iran mines Bitcoin at $1,320 per coin. Sells it at $68,000. A 50x profit margin using state subsidized electricity. 700,000 mining rigs. $7.8 billion crypto shadow economy. All run by the IRGC.

It’s the only revenue stream sanctions can’t touch.

Last time the US hit Iran, Bitcoin’s hashrate dropped 15% overnight. That wasn’t a side effect. That was a proof of concept.

Trump created a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. His son launched a Bitcoin mining company. He wants America to be the “crypto capital of the world.”

You don’t become the crypto capital by competing with a country that mines Bitcoin for $1,320.

You bomb their grid.

Early replies (first 3)

61927885
Im about to drop a dirty bomb, if you are aware of what I mean.

61927888
Not your keys not your wallet bruh

61927889
>>61927857
>It’s the only revenue stream sanctions can’t touch
>casually drops enough bombs to cause you 7b worth of damages overnight
Thanks for playing though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928102
>>61927857
>dirty third world towel heads participate in ponzi scheme
OK

61928107
>>61927857
You can’t mine Bitcoin for that price. Even if your electricity costs are very low, the hardware for a small scale Bitcoin mining warehouse is in the hundreds of thousands of dollars, if not millions at the least. Not taking into account maintenance work, fans for fooling, repair parts since they break even in cold temperatures all the time, etc.

61928126
>>61928107
A dictatorial regime could capture the resources from their plebs and set all this shit up at no cost

61928207
>>61927857
>That wasn’t a side effect. That was a proof of concept.
Thanks ChatGPT

61928279
>>61927857
>>>/pol/529720714
https://www.blockchain.com/explorer/charts/hash-rate
it's not completely without merit

61928597
>>61927857
Wait to see how much it cost the USA to make 1 dollar and how much they sell it for

61928843
>>61928207
Based fellow noticer

61928852
>>61927857
This nigga thinks subsidies are free money that appears out of thin air.

61928869
>>61927857
AI slop post

61929144
BOMB THE BITCOIN MINES

THEY'RE REFINING HIGHER HASHRATES

61931834
>>61927857
not important who mine it
its the same amount

61931863
>>61927857
Iran's case disproves the argument that Bitcoin will die after enough halvings make mining unprofitable. Governments will pay to mine, and bomb each other's data centers to gain an advantage.

61931920
>>61929144
We should bomb the chainlink headquarters

item.title: Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:40 UTC

OP

Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. At least with scams like ICP, the baggies quickly knew they were scammed, the token went to zero and they could move on with their life and try to recover.

Instead Chainlink keeps the scam going for years and years, with new "partnerships" and "news" and "integrations", all while the token does nothing. They give just enough breadcrumbs to keep the baggies holding on desperately for a pump, and they probably keep buying even more LINK tokens as well ensuring they remain poor forever as they wait for a "mega moon" that never comes.

In that way it's actually a more brutal and evil scam, but still funny. It's crazy because all they had to do was buy Bitcoin... or gold, or the S&P 500, or literally anything else.

Early replies (first 3)

61931782
fuck all stinkies :}
hate em all hate em all
think they SUUUUCK xD

61931907
>>61931684
>At least with scams like ICP, the baggies quickly knew they were scammed, the token went to zero and they could move on with their life and try to recover.
Wait a minute. You're telling me I've DCA'd into a scam all these years?

61931931
thanks for keeping this a Chainlink board

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61931939
>>61931782
With comments like these biz should really be renamed to /trash.
Is this supposed to be funny?

61931956
>>61931684
Just glad we can all have a good laugh about it.

61931958
>>61931939
hi there triggered stinkie :}
we see you

61931976
>>61931907
well, you've learned how to dca, next step is to actually buy something worthwhile, like dogshit or bankrupted towel stock

61931997
>>61931976
don't forget GME :} the list of shit that is superior to even link is endless

item.title: Food is so cheap it is practically free. — 112 replies — 420.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Food is so cheap it is practically free.

Open thread • Replies: 112 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:26 UTC

OP

The image posted is in AUD, but for those in the US, Lets assume an hourly wage of $15.

$15 USD in the US can buy 25lbs of rice. which is 45,000 calories. that is astounding.

I work minimum wage (AUS) and after 2 hours of work I can afford an entire weeks worth of food.

the first hour affords me actually nearly a months worth of carbohydrate energy (rice, pasta and potato's)

The second hour affords me 3kg of meat. which is more than enough protein for a week.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Staple_food

very helpful link to understand there is actually not that much food in the entire world to eat for bulk calories/nutrition. there is about 7 different foods to eat. (wheat, rice, corn, potato, meat, dairy and bananas/plantains in certain regions, honorable mention for beans and lentils as well.)

Early replies (first 3)

61926835
People think they're too good for rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables. They deserve to be poor. For the price of two colas you can buy an entire bag of beans which will feed you for weeks. Stop eating corposlop made in a lab that's marked up 10000%, eat real food.

61926840
>>61926822
>eating is so cheap when you eat like a 13th century

61926847
>>61926822
>food is so heckin cheap when you eat like a medieval Chinese peasant!
>updoots to the left!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61927747
>>61926840
>>61926847
Rice with chicken and beans is infinitely more nutritious than Mcdonalds. Cry about it you are weak. Cheap food cooked well is the most beneficial.

61927938
>>61926835
>rice and beans, supplemented with fresh vegetables
this and tuna/deenz+salad is top tier cuisine
As a student I survived so well on this. Best food ever.

61928034
>>61927193
Soil has no very little to do with nutrients. Genetics of the plant has more control over nutrients, so the long lasting bland food that was bred to be that way is the problem. Having regional markets and actual seasons for strains of vegetables that taste good and don't have a long shelf life would contribute to having more nutrients in food.

61928068
>>61926848
Mountain Dew

61928075
>>61926822
>>61926835
Holy fuck. Irredeemably delusional.
Eating only rice and beans like one day you will wake up and have a meltdown about why you did that stupid overly frugal living shit to yourself. That is not gonna be a good day.

61928215
>>61926822
the chemicals and fake meat in Mcmuttslop are literal poison, at least in europe they force these evil fucks to make real food

61928231
>>61926822
Okay great, doesn't matter though when rent is your largest expense by a huge margin

61928338
>>61926867
fpbp

61928341
>>61926867
6 billion pajeets and gooks suggest otherwise

61928406
>>61926822
>calories

Amazing maths there. I better start drinking sunflower oil instead of eating food - that has twice the calories of rice! Just 11lbs of that already beats your 45000 calories bag of rice. Astounding

61928427
>>61926835
>vegetables
anything that isn't potatoes/carrot/cabbage/onion is expensive

61928442
>>61926822
>>61926835
>>61926870
Based. Most first world people just don't have any frugal skills. They fall for the consumer slop and blame there low wage for being broke.

61928596
>>61928075
You don't need to eat beans and rice, you can eat egg rice with minced meat, that's cheap as shit too. Or something like potato with fried chicken. Or Caesar salad. All things you can easily make yourself that cost very little

61928700
>>61926822
McDonalds is garbage
Rice has no nutrition

61928746
>>61927442
Fair enough if you want me to be completely specific, because it really impossible to consume muscle meat without consuming fat, it is always connected unless you are a freak that cuts it off.
But to concede semantics, you need the muscle meat and associated fat, in a ratio of around 70-30 more or less respectively. Organs aren't required, they are nutritious, sometimes overly nutritious to a toxic level in excess.
I don't eat them mainly because they taste nasty. My bones aren't disintigrating, my muscles still grow stronger through training, my shits are 100 times better than before and I have no debilitating chronic diseases. N =1 but it just works.
To keep it /biz/ related, this diet is incredibly cheap and simple and still satiating and appetizing, and I can't recommend it enough. It won't be as cheap as beans and rice but also you won't be sick, constipated, low energy, brain fogged, skinny fat grain slave that ends up spending 200k in hospital bills and medicines.
I spend $400 a month on food, but I do get the much more expensive grass fed ground beef and special cuts from a local farm because I can afford it. If you are in a pinch, those 70/30 beef tubes from walmart could easily get you to survive with minimal effort and probably only $150 a month or so.

61928773
>>61928700
Rice is ok. I wasted way too much of my life studying biochemistry.

Its kinda shit to have incomplete proteins with low bioavailability. You want to have like 1 gram per pound of bodyweight of protein per day. Ideally Cysteine & Taurine.

Look into the cuts of meat we stopped eating in like the late 1800s/early 1900s, these ones are cheap and actually have what we need

61928775
>>61926835
>People think they're too good for rice and beans,
yes, I am rich.

61928813
>>61926822
it's never been easier to avoid eating slop because as well as raising the prices so much food companies also have degraded the quality of ingredients in many snacks and processed foods so they don't taste good anymore.

61928855
>>61927677
retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery, you just have to learn to eat the right amount at the right time, 100g rice 2-3 hours before lifting, 40g rice after lifting to restore glycogen stores, lower carbs at dinner, etc…

61928879
>>61928855
>retard, carbs are key for energy and recovery,
(in your opinion)
after a few months of adaptation, 0 carb btfo carb loading in virtually every single scenario, along with other added benefits. Carbs are just trash. Even animals that eat only plants like cows convert their food into fatty acids through fermentation.

61928887
>>61928773

My go to lunch bowl is rice + eggs + peas (or something else). Very cheap and basically macronutritionally complete.

61928919
>>61926822
>t. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_pDTiFkXgEE

61930720
Feds on 4chan teaching chuds how to cook to prevent collapse wasn't on my 2026 bingo card

61930744
For me, it's oats. They contain:
>Magnesium (muscle and nerve function)
>Iron (oxygen transport)
>Zinc (immune health)
>B vitamins (energy metabolism)
>Manganese (bone formation, metabolism)
>Protein (more than most grains)
>Contains avenanthramides (unique antioxidants in oats)

61930997
As a Dutchman for me it's potatoes, meat, vegetables.

61931628
>sis just eat 2 kg of rice and beans
you're not only paying for calories, you're paying for how compact they are

61931776
>>61926835
Blessed.

61931835
Damn som not even paella?

61931883
>>61926835
They hate you because you speak the truth

61931942
>>61926822
someone actually wrote this whole post

item.content:encoded:
/pmg/ Pirates Metals Goblins

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-03-03 21:38 UTC

OP

ARR!

Early replies (first 3)

61926222
yarrr

61926235
>>61926222
arr har har harr! get ye telescope and check this shipmate!

61926254
Arrgh matey, IQCHADJEETs Silva stock bussin nigga frfr on kirk nigga

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61928859
fuck we're not getting out of the boo box today are we

61928867
>>61928826
I would be surprised if the market was open on Purim in any western city. Or was it the shabbos goys in lambskin aprons doing it? Because "Dude! Blood Moon!".

61928870
>>61928841
Paper is cheaper than physical because you have to pay 20% premiums,,,,people who buy physical are broke retards on k_rk

61928874
Afternoon m8ys
Excellent to see the Captain back at the helm.

61928884
>>61928841
>Only paper silver pumps and dumps like a shit coin.

Talking about shitcoins good Ol' Black Rock one of the biggest real estate assets collectors is starting to liquidate its Bitcoin assets. Doesn't seem like it's a good year to be a crypto bro.

61928922
>>61928867
Blood moon, im gonna COOM!

61928930
>>61928870
The premiums are normally lower. When things are unstable they get very high.

>>61928848
I am talking about $10+ pumps and dumps happening in a single day. That is not natural. If this dropped $15 dollars in a month it would be believable. But you can't even do that because of real supply and demand for silver. And everyone can see when the big dumps happen and figure out who is doing it based on time zone. Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver bombs being dropped on Iran is now added onto your cheap silver tamping problems.

61928997
>>61928930
You're forgetting about Jane Street recently becoming one of the biggest Silver Futures holder right now. You know, the same one being investigated by India and China.

https://coinpedia.org/news/why-is-silver-falling-jane-streets-1-3b-slv-bet-sparks-manipulation-debate/
https://www.reuters.com/world/india/jane-street-deposits-567-million-escrow-accounts-per-india-regulatory-directives-2025-07-14/
https://archive.is/wip/dCGDy

Fuckers are even beating Black Rock. Trying to apply technical analysis to this market while fun is ultimately an exercise in futility if you don't know all of its key players suppressing the price. The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.

61929008
>>61928997
>The only good position in Silver is owning it physically.


The choice of broke retards,,,,no thanks shill

61929016
>>61928930
>Cartels killing Mexican miners and silver

The price dropped $20 yall said this would make it go up $30,,,,lmao yall learned nothing and still claim to nostradamus

61929416
>>61929008
Most of us bought years ago. Myself included. (And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.) The rest are people willing to buy at a loss or are retards. I am here to watch for fun. You are not stressing me out. I wait for Comex to be found empty with glee.

61929468
>>61929416
>And yes I did sell a few ounces when it was over $80 a while ago.
absolutely no shame in that. It's quite literally what silver is for. You buy it when the price is low, build up your stack, and once the GSR closes down like it did recently you sell off no more than half of your stack and convert the FIAT you receive into free gold.
Holding silver forever hoping it will make you rich one day is the same as buying bitcoin nowadays and hoping for the same.
Rinse and repeat.

61929486
About to try and get a refund from TD, been 36 business days and no confirmation of my 4 bars being shipped. They’re gonna try and say some final sale bullshit, but they’ve not held up their part of the “contract”. I stupidly grabbed 5 one ounce silver maples 2 weeks ago, might try to lump that in with the refund (x to doubt for this part being refund) cause I could just run and slurp those 5 locally for same price I paid on TD. Inb4 I get the refund and silver moons before I get the cash back in my account to go buy at my LCS

61929507
I considered selling a little silver for gold recently but quite like that Clive Thompson characters approach if at least waiting for 30to one instead of 50plus. It's nice to have a little pike of silver anyway even if I prefer gold. So atm I'll jyst dca more

61929573
>>61929468
Didn't sell my gold. Not buying gold right now. Maybe in a few years I will buy some bling. Waiting for stock market cheapies and pampering myself this month. Thanks occult retards. You are so predictable. It is fun to find out how many of you blackmailed ziontards own shares stocks I still hold.

61929582
>>61929573
please rewrite that in English and without the schizobabble

61929747
>>61929582
People who got rich have multi generational wealth spend money to cause things to fulfil prophesies because they are bored. It works until people catch on and can figure out when they buy and sell stuff. Purim blood moon at 3:33 AM PST? Massive sell off. Sell some of your stocks and before that day especially institutional investor heavy stocks. Then wait for cheapies in things you were eyeing. Just bought a share of an oil stock that dumped. Makes no sense that it would dump when it was showing the start of a long slow upward trajectory before a classic bull run. And that was before the Iran thing. Oil stock are hardly dumping because they shouldn't be. Aware people are buying today while they dump good stocks for no apparent reason because of dumb rituals. I will wait much longer for other things. I know this could go on for years.

61929770
>>61926208
Anyone else remember AYY lmao coin anon?
Had to sell all his coins because of a family emergency like 2 years ago

I still think about this alien coins he had some fucking cool shit
Poor fucker missed the pump

61929916
>>61929486
Anyone ever tried or had luck with refunding from TD precious metals? Shithawking the chart before I send off my email, knowing today it’ll pull some Talmud magic and moon when I ask for a refund/get refunded

61930008
>>61926208
4 March 2027, we meet and see who has the last laugh. You confident?

61931300
>>61930008

61931346
>>61928874

>>61928544

>>61928696

61931697

61931715

we are fucked

61916113

61918275
Is this still going to 60$? I went all in at 46 because of this thread.

61919240
>>61918275
I Hope return at price of institutional have buy

61919338
>>61892826
>$300
impressive

61919350
>>61918275
>d
the CEO will get his big cut only at $83 dollar share price MINIMUM.
look at warner bros that shit went from below $10 to now beeing baught at $30
If Paypal gets aquired it will be atleast $100

61919773
>>
https://hedgefollow.com/stocks/PYPL
Hope

61921315
>>61919350
I doubt it will go that high.

61923340
>>61923318

61923342
>>61923340

61925981
>>61904727
At the rate they were going, it wasn't going to be a big acquisition, merely a ripple on the pond

61926795
AMZN

61927102
Can someone explain why Paula even exists?? I remember you needed it for eBay back when everything was new. Somehow it morphed into the default asking to payment

61928616
>>61927102
It exists because easy cc payment processor services didn't exist for a long time. You used to give your cc info directly to the web vendors at payment, which eventually got people scammed on shady websites. Paypal was the first of its kind there. The internet has somehow COMPLETELY forgotten that money used to be a complete pain in the ass to move around. Btc, being somewhat clunky now, was actually a huge move in being able to transfer money without oversight or other friction overhead. Now that its been incorporated into the collectivist panopticon, both btc and PayPal are harder to use than just years ago

61930361
ooooooooooooooooooooooooooo

61931059
46,75$
+2,75%
